---
title: "ACC Data Management"
---

::: callout-note
**Coming in a future release.** This chapter documents a planned addition to the AutoDeskR package. The API described here exists in APS but R wrapper functions are not yet available. Track progress at [github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR](https://github.com/paulgovan/AutoDeskR).
:::

# ACC Data Management

The [Data Management (BIM 360 / ACC)](https://aps.autodesk.com/en/docs/data/v2/overview/) API exposes a hierarchical project file structure used by AutoDesk Construction Cloud (ACC) and BIM 360. Unlike the Object Storage Service (OSS) covered in the [Data Management](data-management.qmd) chapter — which uses flat buckets — the ACC hierarchy organises files into **Hubs → Projects → Folders → Items → Versions**.

```
Hub (BIM 360 account or personal hub)
 └── Project
      └── Folder
           └── Item (a file)
                └── Version (a specific revision)
```

This structure enables per-project permissions, version history, and folder-level organisation across large teams.

## Why It Matters

The OSS bucket API is ideal for programmatic file processing pipelines where you manage storage yourself. The ACC hierarchy is required when you need to:

- Navigate files that live inside a BIM 360 or ACC project
- Access a specific version of a model checked in by a team member
- Respect folder-level access controls configured by a project admin

## Planned Functions

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `listHubs(token)` | List all hubs (accounts) accessible to the token |
| `getHub(token, hub_id)` | Retrieve details for a single hub |
| `listProjects(token, hub_id)` | List all projects in a hub |
| `getProject(token, hub_id, project_id)` | Retrieve project details |
| `listFolders(token, project_id, folder_id)` | List subfolders of a folder |
| `listFolderContents(token, project_id, folder_id)` | List items (files) in a folder |
| `listItems(token, project_id)` | List all items in a project |
| `getItem(token, project_id, item_id)` | Retrieve item details |
| `getItemVersions(token, project_id, item_id)` | List all versions of an item |

## Planned Workflow

```{r}
#| eval: false
# (Functions not yet implemented — illustrative only)
token    <- getToken(id, secret, scope = "data:read")

hubs     <- listHubs(token)
hub_id   <- hubs$content$data[[1]]$id

projects <- listProjects(token, hub_id = hub_id)
proj_id  <- projects$content$data[[1]]$id

folders  <- listFolderContents(token, project_id = proj_id, folder_id = "root")
item_id  <- folders$content$data[[1]]$id

versions <- getItemVersions(token, project_id = proj_id, item_id = item_id)
urn      <- versions$content$data[[1]]$relationships$derivatives$data$id
```

Once you have the URN from `getItemVersions()`, it can be passed directly to the [Model Derivative](model-derivative.qmd) functions for translation and metadata extraction.
